text: **Professor Bot:** {Welcome|ようこそ/歓迎} back!
**Professor Bot:** Anna and Penelope are taking a {tour|ツアー/見学} with DC ducks.
**Professor Bot:** In this lesson, we are {looking|探している} for {prepositions|前置詞}.
**Professor Bot:** Listen for in, on, et
**Professor Bot:** under and over some {important|重要な} {prepositions|前置詞} of place.
**Anna:** Penelope, the {Lincoln Memorial|リンカーン記念堂} is coming up on your left.
**Boy:** You're going to {love|大好きだ} my Lincoln Memorial fun {fact|事実/豆知識}.
**Anna:** Oh, please tell us.
**Boy:** On a wall inside the memorial, the word {future|未来} was {carved|彫られた} wrong.
**Anna:** They {carved|彫った} {eucher|誤った綴り}.
**Anna:** Well, we all make {mistakes|間違い}.
**Boy:** Yeah, but this {mistake|間違い} was carved in stone.
**Anna:** You're right.
**Anna:** That is pretty bad.
**Anna:** Here's another {dollar|ドル}.
**Penelope:** More fun facts, please.
**Anna:** I love {riding|乗ること} over {bridges|橋}.
**Anna:** The water looks so {pretty|美しい} from here.
**Anna:** Penelope, this is {amazing|驚くべき}.
**Anna:** A minute ago we were {riding|乗っていた} along the {river|川}.
**Anna:** And now we're riding in the river.
**Anna:** Awesome!
**Penelope:** Hey, we're {passing|通過している} by the {Pentagon|ペンタゴン} on our right.
**Anna:** Penelope, you love riding over the water on a bridge, but I love riding under a bridge in a boat.
**Anna:** Ah no look!
**Penelope:** An {airplane|飛行機} is flying right over our heads!
**Anna:** Wow, this is the closest I've ever been to an airplane in {flight|飛行}.
**Anna:** Well, I-ff- I've been inside a flying airplane, but not outside of an airplane as it flies over my head.
**Penelope:** And now we're driving out of the water.
**Penelope:** I still can't believe this thing drives and {sails|航海する}.
**Anna:** I know.
**Anna:** It's {amazing|驚くべき/素晴らしい|/əˈmeɪzɪŋ/|This is an amazing view!}
**Boy:** After we ride along these {railroad|鉄道|/ˈreɪlroʊd/|Trains run on railroad tracks.} tracks, you'll see my favorite part of the tour.
**Boy:** The US Treasury, where they make
**Boy:** Money.
**Professor Bot:** That young man is making a lot of {money|お金|/ˈmʌni/|I need money to buy a gift.}
**Professor Bot:** Did you hear these {prepositions|前置詞|/ˌprepəˈzɪʃənz/|In and on are common prepositions.}?
**Professor Bot:** As you watch more, look for inside and outside.
**Anna:** Oh look!
**Penelope:** The US {Capitol|国会議事堂|/ˈkæpɪtl/|The Capitol is in Washington, D.C.} is coming up!
**Boy:** I have a great {fun|楽しい/面白い|/fʌn/|We had fun at the party.} fact about the US Capitol.
**Penelope:** What is it?
**Anna:** There are {tunnels|地下通路|/ˈtʌnlz/|Trains pass through tunnels.} under the Capitol.
**Anna:** They connect the Capitol and Office Buildings of {lawmakers|国会議員|/ˈlɔːmeɪkərz/|Lawmakers create new laws.}.
**Anna:** I bet they built them years ago for {secret|秘密の|/ˈsiːkrət/|It's a secret between us.} reasons.
**Boy:** No.
**Boy:** The lawmakers did not want to walk around outside in {bad|悪い/よくない|/bæd/|Bad weather can ruin plans.} weather.
**Anna:** Oh.
**Penelope:** We're back at {Union|連合/統一|/ˈjuːniən/|The union of workers is strong.} Station.
**Boy:** Do you want to know a {creepy|不気味な/恐ろしい|/ˈkriːpi/|That old house looks creepy.} fun fact about Union Station?
**Penelope:** The creepier, the better.
**Boy:** Many years ago, inside Union Station.
**Boy:** There was a {funeral|葬儀|/ˈfjuːnərəl/|A funeral is a ceremony for the dead.} home.
**Anna:** A funeral home?
**Anna:** You mean for {dead|死んだ|/ded/|The plant is dead.} people?
**Anna:** Do you know another kind of funeral home?
**Boy:** No.
**Boy:** Well, I've gotta run.
**Anna:** Thanks for giving us a great {tour|ツアー/観光|/tʊr/|We took a tour of the city.}.
**Anna:** Captain, that was {awesome|素晴らしい/素晴らしい|/ˈɔːsəm/|That concert was awesome!}
**Anna:** And your son is a {great|素晴らしい/優れた|/ɡreɪt/|He is a great student.} {tour|観光/ツアー|/tʊər/|We went on a city tour.} {guide|案内人/ガイド|/ɡaɪd/|The museum guide was helpful.}
**Captain:** What son?
**Anna:** Him
**Captain:** I've never {seen|見た/目にした|/siːn/|I have seen that movie.} that boy in my life.
**Captain:** See ya ladies!
**Professor Bot:** I {learned|学んだ/習得した|/lɜːrnd/|I learned a new skill.} many new fun {facts|事実/情報|/fæks/|These are interesting facts.} in this lesson.
**Professor Bot:** Did you learn about {prepositions|前置詞|/ˌprepəˈzɪʃənz/|Prepositions show relationships.}?
**Professor Bot:** Here's a list of all the {prepositions|前置詞|/ˌprepəˈzɪʃənz/|Prepositions connect words.} used in this lesson.
**Anna:** Wow, that's a lot.
**Anna:** I know, let's not read them.
**Anna:** Let's {sing|歌う/唄う|/sɪŋ/|We like to sing together.} them.
**Anna:** Hit it, boys.
**Anna:** Take it {away|去れ/始めて|/əˈweɪ/|Take it away, musicians!} singers!
**Anna:** Since about, across a head around, over at behind by four, from in like through
**Anna:** Too near on to where
